pages.title: Welcome to Student Device and Account Safety
pages.subtitle: Learn how to protect school devices, student accounts, and classroom technology from misuse or unauthorized access.

pages.title: Why device and account safety matters
pages.text:
School devices and student accounts often provide access to classwork, email, cloud storage, grades, and other school systems.

If a device or account is left unsecured, another person may be able to view information, submit work, change settings, or access school resources without permission.

Good device and account habits help protect students, teachers, and school data.

image.alt: Student using a school device safely

pages.title: School accounts should stay private
pages.text: Each student should use their own school account and keep login details private. Passwords should not be shared with classmates or friends, even if someone says they only need to use the account for a moment. Sharing accounts can expose personal information, schoolwork, and activity history.

pages.title: Shared devices require extra care
pages.text:
Many schools use shared devices such as Chromebooks, tablets, library computers, or lab devices. When using a shared device, students should:
  • Log in only to their own account
  • Log out when finished
  • Close school applications when done
  • Make sure the next user cannot access their files or account
Shared devices are convenient, but they increase the risk of accidental access if users forget to log out.

image.alt: Shared Chromebook or computer in a classroom

pages.title: Lock devices when unattended
pages.text:
A device should never be left unlocked and unattended.

An unlocked device may allow someone else to read messages, change settings, access school systems, or misuse the account.

Teachers and staff should lock devices before stepping away, and students should follow the same habit when appropriate.

image.alt: Locking a school laptop or tablet

pages.title: Be careful with downloads and websites
pages.text:
Students may sometimes click links or download files without realizing the risk.

Unsafe downloads, browser extensions, and suspicious websites can cause problems on school devices.

Only use approved websites, school-assigned tools, and trusted downloads. If something looks unusual, stop and ask a teacher or IT support.

image.alt: Safe browsing and download practices on school devices

pages.title: Do not let others use your signed-in device
pages.text:
Even a short moment of access can create risk if someone else uses a device while you are still signed in.

They may be able to send messages, open files, submit assignments, or view personal school information.

If another person needs access, they should use their own account or go through the proper school process.

image.alt: Risk of letting others use a signed-in school device

pages.title: What to do if something seems wrong
pages.text:
If a student account or device may be compromised:
  1. Stop using the device if needed
  2. Log out or lock the account if possible
  3. Tell a teacher, administrator, or IT support right away
  4. Change the password if instructed
Quick reporting can help prevent bigger problems.

image.alt: Reporting a school device or account issue
